message:
chore(beads): tick 361 — file ft-326s9 + ft-jqvg5 as adjacent follow-ups

Filed two P3 follow-up beads surfacing the §6.1 findings from
ft-xbnl0.2.4's completion-evidence doc:

- ft-326s9 (bug) — "Fix bundle_acceptor_connector_mtls test —
  config shape contradicts production logic"
  - Pre-existing test failure (authored 2026-04-10, 8 days before
    this session). Test constructs a client_cfg with Mtls auth but
    no client_ca_path, expects bundle construction to succeed.
    Production logic correctly returns MissingClientCaPath — which
    ft-xbnl0.2.4 tick 337 positive-guard explicitly pins as intended.
  - Fix options enumerated: (1) populate client_ca_path, (2)
    restructure bundle's Mtls handling, (3) deprecate the test.

- ft-jqvg5 (task) — "Resolve frankenterm-core clippy -D warnings gate"
  - 17 clippy errors across 7 files (ipc.rs, robot_sdk_contracts.rs,
    runtime.rs, snapshot_engine.rs, ui_query.rs, workflows/engine.rs,
    workflows/runner.rs) — none in ft-xbnl0.2.4's scope.
  - Recommends coordination with file owners before fixes per
    AGENTS.md "don't disturb other agents' work".

Both linked as `related` to ft-xbnl0.2.4 so they surface alongside
it during triage. Both are P3 — not blocking closure.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"ft-2h5wv.5","title":"[ft-2h5wv][support] Add remaining external public MuxPool explicit-Cx cancellation coverage","description":"Support slice for ft-2h5wv: add external/public warmed-pooled behavioral coverage proving get_lines_with_cx, write_to_pane_with_cx, and send_paste_with_cx fail fast for a pre-cancelled Cx and emit no extra post-handshake request frames. Scope: crates/frankenterm-core/tests/vendored_async_contract_behavioral.rs only. Validation: rch-backed targeted vendored/asupersync behavioral test lane.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-01T17:14:12.376821Z","created_by":"GoldenPlateau","updated_at":"2026-04-01T18:16:37.305748Z","closed_at":"2026-04-01T18:16:37.305569Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["asupersync","support","test-coverage"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-2h5wv.5","depends_on_id":"ft-2h5wv","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-01T17:14:12.376821Z","created_by":"GoldenPlateau","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2677,"issue_id":"ft-2h5wv.5","author":"GoldenPlateau","text":"GoldenPlateau started ft-2h5wv.5 in crates/frankenterm-core/tests/vendored_async_contract_behavioral.rs only. Scope is warmed pooled explicit-Cx cancellation coverage for get_lines_with_cx, write_to_pane_with_cx, and send_paste_with_cx; no vendored production-file edits.","created_at":"2026-04-01T17:15:51Z"},{"id":2678,"issue_id":"ft-2h5wv.5","author":"GoldenPlateau","text":"Validation update: local hygiene is clean for ft-2h5wv.5 (rustfmt --edition 2024 --check + git diff --check on crates/frankenterm-core/tests/vendored_async_contract_behavioral.rs). Remote validation is in flight via rch on worker vmi1167313 using isolated CARGO_HOME=.cargo-rch-goldenplateau-d and CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target/rch-goldenplateau-ft-2h5wv5 against the vendored_async_contract_behavioral mux_pool_ subset.","created_at":"2026-04-01T17:25:30Z"},{"id":2679,"issue_id":"ft-2h5wv.5","author":"GoldenPlateau","text":"Remote validation attempt on vmi1167313 is infra-invalid and not counted as code evidence: rch reported 'can't find integration-test vendored_async_contract_behavioral' even though the local file exists. Treating this as the same remote sync defect class seen on earlier workers, not as a source regression. Retrying on a fresh remote cargo home/target dir.","created_at":"2026-04-01T17:25:42Z"},{"id":2680,"issue_id":"ft-2h5wv.5","author":"GoldenPlateau","text":"Third validation attempt is also infra-invalid and not counted as evidence: after temporarily draining the repeatedly-bad worker vmi1167313, rch immediately fail-opened local with 'all workers failed preflight checks'. I am killing that local fallback to stay within the repo's rch-only build policy.","created_at":"2026-04-01T17:29:00Z"},{"id":2682,"issue_id":"ft-2h5wv.5","author":"GoldenPlateau","text":"Completed with remote validation. Added warmed pooled explicit-Cx cancellation coverage for get_lines_with_cx, write_to_pane_with_cx, and send_paste_with_cx in crates/frankenterm-core/tests/vendored_async_contract_behavioral.rs (b23w/b23x/b23y). Local hygiene stayed clean (rustfmt --edition 2024 --check and git diff --check). Final remote evidence passed via rch on worker vmi1149989: cargo test -p frankenterm-core --test vendored_async_contract_behavioral --features vendored,asupersync-runtime mux_pool_ -- --nocapture => 9 passed, 0 failed, 42 filtered out. Only warning was the pre-existing dead_code warning for save_restore_checkpoint at crates/frankenterm-core/src/session_restore.rs:440. During validation I also identified repo-local rch sync failures from recursive diagnostic snapshot dirs and updated .rch/config.toml excludes to cover beads_diag_snapshot*/ and bddiag_*/ so remote sync could complete without touching user snapshot data.","created_at":"2026-04-01T18:16:22Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-2h5wv.6","title":"[ft-2h5wv][support] Add internal mux_pool explicit-Cx precancelled counter regressions","description":"Support slice for ft-2h5wv: add internal unit regressions in crates/frankenterm-core/src/vendored/mux_pool.rs only. Scope is pre-cancelled explicit-Cx behavior for get_lines_with_cx, write_to_pane_with_cx, and send_paste_with_cx, asserting PoolError::Cancelled fast-fail plus pool stats/counter invariants (no connection creation, no acquire timeout increments, no recovery attempts). This intentionally avoids crates/frankenterm-core/tests/vendored_async_contract_behavioral.rs, which is already owned by ft-2h5wv.5. Validation target: rch-backed focused vendored/asupersync lib test lane when remote preflight permits; local hygiene only otherwise.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"TealLotus","created_at":"2026-04-01T17:49:25.520334Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-11T02:27:46.460632Z","closed_at":"2026-04-11T02:27:46.459381Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["asupersync","support","test-coverage"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-2h5wv.6","depends_on_id":"ft-2h5wv","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-01T17:49:25.520334Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2681,"issue_id":"ft-2h5wv.6","author":"TealLotus","text":"Remote validation update (2026-04-01): rch lane on worker vmi1227854 with CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target/rch-ft-2h5wv6 did not yield code evidence. rustc for frankenterm-core lib test was SIGKILLed after a 536s cold compile (638s total incl. sync), so this run is treated as infra-invalid rather than a source failure. Starting one bounded retry with --no-default-features plus vendored/asupersync-runtime-only feature selection to reduce compile surface and memory pressure.","created_at":"2026-04-01T18:05:26Z"},{"id":2684,"issue_id":"ft-2h5wv.6","author":"GoldenPlateau","text":"Useful infra note from ft-2h5wv.5 closeout: repeated rch sync failures were caused by recursive diagnostic snapshot trees in the repo root, specifically beads_diag_snapshot* and bddiag_*. Adding those patterns to .rch/config.toml transfer excludes eliminated the rsync max-path fail-open and allowed a clean remote run on vmi1149989. If your bounded retry is still transport-failing before Cargo starts, retry against current tree so you inherit that exclude fix.","created_at":"2026-04-01T18:17:43Z"},{"id":2688,"issue_id":"ft-2h5wv.6","author":"TealLotus","text":"Validation lane interrupted on 2026-04-01 after user clarified a strict no-tokio policy override. I stopped the in-flight rch retry rather than keep validating under an assumption that conflicts with the current directive. Slice remains otherwise unchanged; no new code bug was found in mux_pool itself during the audit.","created_at":"2026-04-01T18:28:47Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-2oph2.2","title":"[ft-2oph2][perf] Reuse chunked scan buffer for definitive trigger scan","description":"# Goal
Eliminate duplicate full-buffer accumulation in the chunked scan pipeline when both trigger scanning and compression are enabled.

## Scope
- File: `crates/frankenterm-core/src/scan_pipeline.rs`
- Reuse the existing uncompressed chunk buffer as the definitive flush-time trigger input whenever compression is enabled, instead of also appending the same bytes into `trigger_data_buffer`.
- Preserve exact batch/chunk trigger parity and compression behavior.
- Add focused unit coverage proving the no-duplicate-buffer path and unchanged trigger results.

## Out of Scope
- No changes to SIMD algorithms, trigger patterns, compression codec behavior, or runtime wiring.
- No changes to unrelated runtime/event/storage files.

## Validation
- `rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenterm-core --lib scan_pipeline -- --nocapture`
- `rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenterm-core --test proptest_scan_pipeline -- --nocapture`
- `rch exec -- cargo bench -p frankenterm-core --bench scan_pipeline --no-run`

## Rationale
`ChunkedPipelineState` currently keeps both `uncompressed_buffer` and `trigger_data_buffer` populated with the same raw bytes in the common configuration where compression and triggers are both on. This inflates memory traffic and buffer growth on the hot path without changing behavior.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"TealLotus","created_at":"2026-03-31T18:02:43.603376Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-01T17:20:17.917970Z","closed_at":"2026-04-01T17:20:17.917717Z","close_reason":"Implemented chunked scan buffer reuse, strengthened compression-enabled trigger parity tests, and validated via rch-backed scan_pipeline/proptest/bench coverage; remaining repo-wide failures were unrelated.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"ft-326s9","title":"Fix bundle_acceptor_connector_mtls test — config shape contradicts production logic","description":"# Pre-existing mTLS test failure

## Background
Discovered during ft-xbnl0.2.4 tick 359 verification work. The test
`distributed::tests::bundle_acceptor_connector_mtls` at
`crates/frankenterm-core/src/distributed.rs:3805` fails reproducibly with:

    client bundle: MissingClientCaPath

## Reproduction
```
cargo test -p frankenterm-core --features distributed \
    --lib bundle_acceptor_connector_mtls -- --nocapture
```

## Root cause
The test constructs a `client_cfg` with `auth_mode =
DistributedAuthMode::Mtls` but without `client_ca_path` populated:

```rust
let mut client_cfg = DistributedConfig::default();
client_cfg.enabled = true;
client_cfg.auth_mode = DistributedAuthMode::Mtls;
client_cfg.tls.enabled = true;
client_cfg.tls.cert_path = Some(client_cert.path().display().to_string());
client_cfg.tls.key_path = Some(client_key.path().display().to_string());
// client_ca_path intentionally left as None.
```

Then it calls `build_tls_bundle(&client_cfg, Some(ca_cert.path()))`. Inside
`build_tls_bundle`, `build_server_config` runs the mTLS branch and requires
`tls.client_ca_path` (the cert used to verify INCOMING client certs). It is
None → `DistributedTlsError::MissingClientCaPath`.

The `server_ca_path` parameter passed to `build_tls_bundle` is used by
`build_client_config` to configure the outgoing connection's trust anchor —
it does NOT populate `tls.client_ca_path`.

## The tension
- `ft-xbnl0.2.4` tick 337 test `build_tls_bundle_rejects_mtls_without_client_ca_path`
  explicitly pins "Mtls auth without client_ca_path → `MissingClientCaPath`"
  as the intended production contract.
- The failing test expects the opposite (that this config shape succeeds).

## Options
1. Fix the test: set `client_cfg.tls.client_ca_path = Some(ca_cert.path().display().to_string())` alongside the existing cert/key.
2. Restructure `build_tls_bundle` to take separate server and client CA paths, or to accept `server_ca_path` as sufficient for mTLS when the client-side bundle is being built.
3. Deprecate the failing test if the config shape was only ever tentative.

## Attribution
- Authored: jemanuel on 2026-04-10 (commit 147b22176).
- Never fixed or touched since.
- Observed failing on 2026-04-18 during ft-xbnl0.2.4 verification sweep.

## Not a blocker for ft-xbnl0.2.4
The bead-scoped check script (`scripts/check_ft_xbnl0_2_4.sh`) uses `--lib tls_`
filter which does not catch this test (the name has `mtls` with no `tls_`
substring). All 73 tests in that filter pass. The failure surfaces only on
broader `--lib distributed::tests::` filter.

See: `docs/ft-xbnl0-2-4-completion-evidence.md §6.1.2`.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"jemanuel","created_at":"2026-04-18T18:04:36.191788Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-18T18:05:03.144028Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["mtls","pre-existing","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-326s9","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.2.4","type":"related","created_at":"2026-04-18T18:05:03.143481Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-682n6","title":"Migrate ~50 tokio-based async tests to asupersync runtime (ft-22x4r tracked)","description":"# Goal
Convert ~50 tests using #[tokio::test] to work with the asupersync runtime. These tests fail at runtime with: task::spawn called outside of Runtime::block_on context. Originally tracked as ft-22x4r.

# Background
The asupersync migration replaced tokio, but ~50 tests still use #[tokio::test]. Count verified 2026-04-06: 50 in .rs files (excluding .bak). Most are in *_labruntime.rs test files (2 per file across ~25 files) plus 2 in dependency_eradication.rs.

# Design
## Discovery (exact count at implementation time)
1. grep -r '#\[tokio::test\]' crates/ --include='*.rs' | grep -v '.bak' > /tmp/tokio_tests.txt
2. Count and categorize by file pattern:
   - *_labruntime.rs files: ~25 files × 2 tests = ~50 tests (async smoke + basic scenario)
   - dependency_eradication.rs: 2 tests
3. For each, identify tokio primitives used: spawn, sleep, channels, timeout

## Migration strategy
- *_labruntime.rs pattern: These all follow the same template (2 tests per file). Once one is migrated, the rest are mechanical.
  Replace: #[tokio::test] with #[test] + runtime_compat::block_on(async { ... })
  Or: if asupersync provides a test macro, use that
- Replace tokio::spawn -> runtime_compat spawn equivalent
- Replace tokio::time::sleep -> runtime_compat::sleep
- Replace tokio channels -> runtime_compat channels

## Batch approach
Since *_labruntime.rs files follow a uniform pattern, write a migration helper that does the mechanical replacement, then verify each file compiles.

## Structured Logging
- Migration log: file, old_macro, new_macro, primitives_replaced, compile_status

# Acceptance Criteria
1. Zero #[tokio::test] in .rs files (verified by grep, excluding .bak).
2. All ~50 previously-failing tests now pass.
3. No semantic changes — same assertions.
4. Total test count preserved.
5. No new tokio dependencies.
6. Commit message lists affected files.

# Implementation Notes
- runtime_compat.rs provides bridge primitives.
- The *_labruntime.rs naming convention suggests these are lab/smoke tests for async behavior.
- This bead unblocks ft-xbnl0.2 (async cutover epic).
- Coordinate with ft-n2j5w (MCP test fix) if any MCP tests also use tokio::test.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":240,"created_at":"2026-04-06T18:58:21.227738Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-07T02:55:15.155157Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T02:55:15.154811Z","close_reason":"Verified: zero actual #[tokio::test] annotations remain in crates/frankenterm-core/. All 50 occurrences found by grep are in comments/docstrings/string literals describing the migration that was already done. The *_labruntime.rs files already use #[test] + RuntimeFixture::current_thread().block_on(). No code changes needed.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["asupersync","migration","testing"]}
 {"id":"ft-akx00","title":"[EPIC] Eliminate verified first-party placeholders and stubs","description":"# Goal
Eliminate every verified first-party placeholder, stub, mock-like runtime branch, and misleading partial implementation surfaced by the 2026-04-02 audit.

Every in-scope item must end in exactly one of two states:
1. Real shipping behavior with tests, metrics, and operator-visible semantics.
2. Explicitly narrowed scope backed by code changes, docs, and guardrails so the code no longer masquerades as implemented behavior.

# Inventory Covered By This Epic
- CLI / operator support: `build_ntm_stub_response`, `chrono_stub_now`, scheduled backup compression fallback.
- Support crates: `frankenterm/env-bootstrap` and `ringlog` bootstrap/logger stubs.
- Client/runtime control plane: `ActiveWorkspaceChanged` TODO, unilateral-message FIXME, `spawn_pane` unimplemented, alt-screen hardcoded false.
- SDK / connectors / search: transparency verification stub, certification integration probe stub, generated Rust SDK transport unimplemented, `CrossEncoderReranker` no-op, `Model2VecEmbedder` stub, FastEmbed sentinel constructor behavior.
- Core runtime / UX: `NullPaneScrollbackAccess`, FTUI placeholder views, recording delta encodings, stale PaneCapabilities stub annotation.
- Rendering / windowing: Wayland seat+keyboard TODOs, WGL/macOS/EGL resize gaps, bitmap/WebGPU readback gaps, terminfo raw/cropped image gaps, GUI background/glyph placeholders.
- Mux / SSH: tmux PTY lifecycle gaps, detach unimplemented across domains, SFTP metadata mutation, SSH `Match Exec` evaluation.

# Scope Boundary
Only first-party source is in scope. Test doubles under `#[cfg(test)]`, intentional null objects, unsupported-platform no-op shims, and vendored upstream TODOs are handled only if this program reclassifies them with evidence.","design":"# Execution Strategy
1. Start with inventory discipline so no suspect disappears mid-stream.
2. Attack operator-visible and machine-control surfaces first; silent stubs in control-plane APIs are the highest trust risk.
3. Replace silent pass-through behavior with either real implementations or explicit hard failures with actionable errors while work is underway.
4. Prefer shared primitives over leaf fixes. Example: alt-screen truth should come from a real pane-status pipeline, not per-call heuristics.
5. Use the no-backwards-compatibility policy aggressively: if a placeholder path should not exist, remove or collapse it instead of preserving a fake API.
6. Every completed child bead must remove the misleading placeholder signal from code and add regression tests proving the real behavior.","acceptance_criteria":"- Every verified suspect from the audit is mapped to a child bead under this epic.
- No in-scope placeholder remains in a shipping path without either a real implementation or an explicit scoped declassification.
- Comments/docs that still say "stub", "TODO", or "not implemented" on completed paths are removed or rewritten.
- Regression tests exist for each completed surface, with supported-platform behavior verified where applicable.
- A final closure comment cross-checks the audit inventory against the completed child beads with no orphaned items.
- Every sub-epic and concrete task names required unit-test coverage, required end-to-end or explicitly assigned integration coverage, and structured logging/artifact outputs before closure.
- Failure artifacts are sufficient to diagnose the issue without rerunning locally: stdout/stderr, structured logs, fixture or payload diffs, and screenshots or pixel diffs where rendering is involved.","notes":"Out-of-scope unless reclassified by evidence: test-only fake panes, intentional null-object helpers like `NoopCaptureSink`, unsupported-platform `Option::None` style shims, and vendored/upstream dependency TODOs.

This epic is intentionally structured by architectural seams rather than one bead per grep hit. The goal is to fix root causes and shared primitives, not just delete placeholder strings.

## Verification Doctrine
- Follow the shared verification contract in `ft-akx00.8`.
- Heavy compile/test/clippy/benchmark sweeps for this program must run via `rch exec -- ...`.
- Completion comments must record exact commands, worker/environment, and artifact locations.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:27:33.773255Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-03T02:05:48.911043Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e-tests","integration-tests","placeholder-remediation","quality-gate","rendering","stub-audit","test-coverage","test-logging","user-impact"],"comments":[{"id":2740,"issue_id":"ft-akx00","author":"jemanuel","text":"This epic is the execution wrapper for the 2026-04-02 placeholder/stub audit. The child tracks are ordered to minimize wasted work:
- machine-control and client behavior first,
- search/connector trust next,
- core runtime and FTUI after shared state surfaces are real,
- rendering/backend parity after higher-risk control paths,
- mux/SSH operational gaps in parallel where isolated.

Future-self rule: if a new suspect appears while working these beads, either attach it to the appropriate child bead or create a new child under this epic immediately. Do not let the inventory drift back into ad hoc comments.","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:28:55Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-akx00.1","title":"Maintain closure ledger for placeholder-remediation program","description":"# Purpose
Maintain the closure ledger for this remediation program so the audit remains complete and falsifiable.

This bead owns the suspect matrix, scope decisions, and final proof that every audited placeholder ended in a deliberate outcome. It exists to prevent the classic failure mode where implementation starts but the inventory stops being tracked.

# Deliverables
- A suspect-by-suspect ledger mapping each audited item to a child bead and final disposition.
- Explicit documentation for any item that gets reclassified as intentional or out-of-scope.
- Final cross-check before the parent epic closes.","design":"Treat the original audit as an immutable input set. For each suspect, record:
- exact file/function/branch,
- why it was considered placeholder behavior,
- which remediation bead owns it,
- what proof is required to declare it closed.

If a task discovers that the optimal fix is deletion or API collapse, record that explicitly here so future readers know it was a principled simplification rather than an omission.","acceptance_criteria":"- The ledger names every verified suspect from the 2026-04-02 audit.
- Each suspect points to exactly one owning child bead or an explicit documented reclassification.
- The final comment on this bead reports any deltas between the initial inventory and the executed work.
- The ledger records not only ownership but also the verification surface for each audited placeholder: unit tests, E2E/integration coverage, and artifact expectations.","notes":"This is a program-control task, not a code task. It should stay open until the end of the entire remediation campaign.

Keep this ledger synchronized with the shared verification contract in `ft-akx00.8` so future implementers can see both who owns a placeholder and how correctness will be proven.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SageHorse","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:28:55.213472Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-03T14:25:13.664461Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["placeholder-remediation","quality-gate","stub-audit","test-coverage","test-logging"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-akx00.1","depends_on_id":"ft-akx00","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:28:55.213472Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2741,"issue_id":"ft-akx00.1","author":"jemanuel","text":"Initial ledger categories:
- Control plane/client: NTM stub response, client workspace/unilateral handling, client spawn/alt-screen, robot SDK transport.
- Operator support: chrono stub, env-bootstrap/ringlog stubs, scheduled backup compression.
- Search/connectors: transparency verification, certification probe, reranker, model2vec, fastembed sentinel behavior.
- Core runtime/UX: NullPaneScrollbackAccess, recording encodings, FTUI placeholder views, stale PaneCapabilities stub comment.
- Rendering/backends: Wayland seat+keyboard, WGL/macOS/EGL resize, bitmap/WebGPU readback, terminfo raw/crop images, background sizing, custom glyph placeholders.
- Mux/SSH: tmux PTY lifecycle, detach semantics, SFTP metadata, SSH Match Exec.","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:28:55Z"},{"id":2772,"issue_id":"ft-akx00.1","author":"jemanuel","text":"Inventory-to-bead ledger for the 2026-04-02 placeholder audit:

Control plane / client / SDK
- `build_ntm_stub_response` fake `"status": "stub"` payload -> `ft-akx00.2.1`
- `ActiveWorkspaceChanged` TODO + unilateral-message FIXME -> `ft-akx00.2.2`
- `ClientDomain::spawn_pane` unimplemented + `ClientPane::is_alt_screen_active()` hardcoded false -> `ft-akx00.2.3`
- generated Rust SDK transport `unimplemented!("transport not wired")` -> `ft-akx00.2.4`

Operator support / persistence utilities
- `chrono_stub_now()` -> `ft-akx00.3.1`
- `env-bootstrap` / `ringlog` stub + no-op logger -> `ft-akx00.3.2`
- scheduled backup compression placeholder -> `ft-akx00.3.3`

Connector / search
- connector transparency verification stub -> `ft-akx00.4.1`
- certification integration probe stub -> `ft-akx00.4.2`
- `CrossEncoderReranker` identity/no-op stub -> `ft-akx00.4.3`
- `Model2VecEmbedder` stub + FastEmbed sentinel constructor -> `ft-akx00.4.4`

Core runtime / FTUI / policy
- `NullPaneScrollbackAccess` placeholder -> `ft-akx00.5.1`
- `DeltaEncoding::Diff` / `Repeat` placeholders -> `ft-akx00.5.2`
- FTUI placeholder view bodies -> `ft-akx00.5.3`
- stale PaneCapabilities stub annotation in `policy.rs` -> `ft-akx00.5.4`

Rendering / windowing
- Wayland seat + keyboard TODO/unimplemented paths -> `ft-akx00.6.1`
- WGL/macOS/EGL resize + framebuffer gaps -> `ft-akx00.6.2`
- bitmap/WebGPU texture readback gaps -> `ft-akx00.6.3`
- terminfo raw/cropped image rendering gaps -> `ft-akx00.6.4`
- GUI background sizing + custom glyph placeholder rendering -> `ft-akx00.6.5`

Mux / SSH
- tmux PTY `try_wait()` + kill placeholders -> `ft-akx00.7.1`
- detach unimplemented across domains -> `ft-akx00.7.2`
- SFTP metadata mutation placeholder -> `ft-akx00.7.3`
- SSH `Match Exec` omission -> `ft-akx00.7.4`

Deliberate exclusions from this epic unless later reclassified by evidence:
- test-only fake panes and other `#[cfg(test)]` doubles
- intentional null-object helpers like `NoopCaptureSink`
- unsupported-platform no-op shims used only to represent platform absence
- vendored/upstream TODOs outside first-party source","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:33:17Z"},{"id":2798,"issue_id":"ft-akx00.1","author":"jemanuel","text":"Verification-matrix reminder:
- Shared contract bead: `ft-akx00.8`
- Every concrete child under `ft-akx00` now requires both comprehensive unit tests and a dedicated or explicitly assigned E2E/integration scenario with structured logs and artifacts.
- Completion evidence for future implementation beads should cite the exact proposed script name (or renamed final equivalent), exact commands, artifact root, and whether heavy validation ran via `rch`.","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:04:52Z"},{"id":2813,"issue_id":"ft-akx00.1","author":"SageHorse","text":"Ledger update for `ft-akx00.5.2` (closed 2026-04-03):

Suspect:
- `crates/frankenterm-core/src/recording.rs`: dormant `DeltaEncoding::Diff` / `DeltaEncoding::Repeat` placeholder branches plus `DiffOp` placeholder type.

Disposition:
- Closed by API collapse, not by implementing fake delta machinery. Recording WAR v1 now exposes only `DeltaEncoding::Full(Vec<u8>)`, and the spec was updated to say so explicitly. This is a principled narrowing of scope, not an omission.

Proof captured:
- Code/docs updated: `crates/frankenterm-core/src/recording.rs`, `crates/frankenterm-core/tests/proptest_recording.rs`, `docs/recording-format-spec.md`.
- Regression: serde test now rejects the removed legacy placeholder variants.
- Targeted verification passed: `rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenterm-core --test proptest_recording` (47 tests).
- Local formatting proof: `rustfmt --edition 2024 --check crates/frankenterm-core/src/recording.rs crates/frankenterm-core/tests/proptest_recording.rs crates/frankenterm-core/tests/proptest_reranker.rs`.

Verification notes / non-bead blockers:
- `rch exec -- cargo check --workspace --all-targets` remains blocked by remote worker env drift (`x11-xcb.pc` missing on worker `vmi1149989`).
- `rch exec -- cargo clippy -p frankenterm-core --all-targets -- -D warnings` remains blocked by existing failures in `frankenterm/config/derive` and `frankenterm/dynamic/derive`.
- Fresh `rch exec -- cargo check -p frankenterm-core --all-targets` got past earlier test exhaustiveness drift and now fails only on unrelated bench debt in `crates/frankenterm-core/benches/input_latency_bench.rs` and `crates/frankenterm-core/benches/spike_comparison.rs`.

Cross-reference:
- Owning bead: `ft-akx00.5.2`
- Verification contract bead: `ft-akx00.8`

Follow-up for this ledger bead: accumulate the same disposition/proof block for each remaining child bead so the parent epic can be closed against a falsifiable matrix rather than scattered thread history.","created_at":"2026-04-03T14:25:13Z"}]}

3. Trigger release.yml workflow

# Acceptance Criteria
1. Git tag v0.1.0 exists with annotated release notes.
2. GitHub Release page has downloadable binaries.
3. Release notes are honest — no feature is listed as working unless it passes end-to-end.
4.  {"id":"ft-gofz5.3","title":"Add CI cargo-install smoke test that runs on every push to main","description":"# Goal
Add a CI job to .github/workflows/ci.yml that runs cargo install from the repo and verifies the binary works, catching install regressions early.

# Design
- New job in ci.yml: install-smoke
- Matrix: ubuntu-latest (x86_64), macos-latest (arm64)
- Steps: checkout → install Rust nightly → cargo install --path crates/frankenterm → ft --version → ft doctor --json
- Timeout: 30 minutes (the build is large)
- Cache: cargo registry + target dir for speed

# Acceptance Criteria
1. CI job passes on both platforms.
2. Job fails if ft binary doesn't produce valid --version output.
3. Job completes in under 20 minutes with caching.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":60,"created_at":"2026-04-06T18:56:11.405247Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-11T02:01:54.765078Z","closed_at":"2026-04-11T02:01:54.763741Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","regression","release"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-gofz5.3","depends_on_id":"ft-gofz5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:56:11.405247Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-gofz5.3","depends_on_id":"ft-gofz5.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:56:15.917903Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-grz8r","title":"[EPIC] Eliminate verified first-party placeholders and stubs","description":"# Goal
Eliminate every verified first-party placeholder, stub, mock-like runtime branch, and misleading partial implementation surfaced by the 2026-04-02 audit.

Every in-scope item must end in exactly one of two states:
1. Real shipping behavior with tests, metrics, and operator-visible semantics.
2. Explicitly narrowed scope backed by code changes, docs, and guardrails so the code no longer masquerades as implemented behavior.

# Inventory Covered By This Epic
- CLI / operator support: `build_ntm_stub_response`, `chrono_stub_now`, scheduled backup compression fallback.
- Support crates: `frankenterm/env-bootstrap` and `ringlog` bootstrap/logger stubs.
- Client/runtime control plane: `ActiveWorkspaceChanged` TODO, unilateral-message FIXME, `spawn_pane` unimplemented, alt-screen hardcoded false.
- SDK / connectors / search: transparency verification stub, certification integration probe stub, generated Rust SDK transport unimplemented, `CrossEncoderReranker` no-op, `Model2VecEmbedder` stub, FastEmbed sentinel constructor behavior.
- Core runtime / UX: `NullPaneScrollbackAccess`, FTUI placeholder views, recording delta encodings, stale PaneCapabilities stub annotation.
- Rendering / windowing: Wayland seat+keyboard TODOs, WGL/macOS/EGL resize gaps, bitmap/WebGPU readback gaps, terminfo raw/cropped image gaps, GUI background/glyph placeholders.
- Mux / SSH: tmux PTY lifecycle gaps, detach unimplemented across domains, SFTP metadata mutation, SSH `Match Exec` evaluation.

# Scope Boundary
Only first-party source is in scope. Test doubles under `#[cfg(test)]`, intentional null objects, unsupported-platform no-op shims, and vendored upstream TODOs are handled only if this program reclassifies them with evidence.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:26:38.047585Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-11T02:31:29.254960Z","closed_at":"2026-04-11T02:31:29.253673Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["placeholder-remediation","stub-audit"]}
+{"id":"ft-jqvg5","title":"Resolve frankenterm-core clippy -D warnings gate","description":"# Workspace-level clippy gate

## Background
Discovered during ft-xbnl0.2.4 tick 356 verification sweep. The
`frankenterm-core` crate reports **17 clippy errors** when run with
`-D warnings`:

    cargo clippy --no-deps -p frankenterm-core \\
        --features distributed,asupersync-runtime,web \\
        --lib --tests -- -D warnings

All 17 errors are in files **unrelated to ft-xbnl0.2.4**:

- `src/ipc.rs`
- `src/robot_sdk_contracts.rs`
- `src/runtime.rs`
- `src/snapshot_engine.rs`
- `src/ui_query.rs`
- `src/workflows/engine.rs`
- `src/workflows/runner.rs`

These are from recent commits by other agents.

## Why not fix in ft-xbnl0.2.4
AGENTS.md explicitly instructs "NEVER ... disturb in ANY way the work of
other agents." The violations may be:
- awaiting `#[allow(...)]` annotations from their owners
- pending intentional restructures
- already planned for cleanup

Touching them without coordination could override the owners' planned
solutions.

## Fix categories observed (examples)
- `clippy::duration-suboptimal-units` — `Duration::from_millis(2_000)` should be `from_secs(2)`
- `clippy::redundant-closure` — `|| crate::foo()` should just be `crate::foo`
- `clippy::semicolon-if-nothing-returned` — trailing unit-valued expression without semicolon
- Several others (see `cargo clippy` output at time of fix)

## Suggested approach
For each file, coordinate with the owning agent (via Agent Mail or by checking
recent git history for active work) before applying fixes. Prefer:
1. Fixing real issues over `#[allow(...)]` annotations.
2. Single-file commits so each fix is easy to review.
3. Running `ubs` on each file after the clippy fix to avoid introducing
   new bugs in the cleanup.

## Not a blocker for ft-xbnl0.2.4
The ft-xbnl0.2.4-scoped files (`src/distributed.rs`, `src/metrics.rs`,
`src/runtime_compat.rs`, `tests/web.rs`, `tests/ft_xbnl0_2_4_*.rs`) produce
zero clippy warnings.

See: `docs/ft-xbnl0-2-4-completion-evidence.md §6.1.1`.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"jemanuel","created_at":"2026-04-18T18:04:55.194692Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-18T18:05:03.540892Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["clippy","code-quality","workspace-gate"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-jqvg5","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.2.4","type":"related","created_at":"2026-04-18T18:05:03.540304Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-kfkyi","title":"DistributedHttpClient must not transparently follow 3xx redirects","description":"# Security: DistributedHttpClient transparent redirect following

## Background
Discovered during ft-xbnl0.2.4 tick 349 verification work. `DistributedHttpClient`
(built on the default asupersync HTTP client config) **follows 3xx redirects
transparently** by default. A 302 with an arbitrary `Location:` header is
followed without caller visibility.

Current behavior is pinned by `distributed_http_client_3xx_redirect_current_behavior_snapshot`
in `crates/frankenterm-core/src/distributed.rs` (tests module) — the test
asserts `Err` on a 302 whose Location is unresolvable, and explicitly
documents that assertion flipping is the trigger for this bead's work.

## Security concern
`DistributedHttpClient` is used for distributed node-to-node RPC (health
checks, event forwarding). Peer URLs are supposed to point at trusted
nodes. Transparent redirect following means:

- A compromised peer can respond `302 Location: http://attacker.com/` →
  next request body is sent to attacker (including event data, pane
  captures, auth tokens).
- Even without compromise, a misconfigured peer with a redirect loop
  could cause unbounded work.

## Fix
Configure `RedirectPolicy::none()` at the `DistributedHttpClient`
construction site — see `crates/frankenterm-core/src/distributed.rs`
around `DistributedHttpClient::new()` and `DistributedHttpClient::plaintext()`.

This forces the caller to explicitly handle (or ignore) the redirect.

## Verification
After the fix:
1. `distributed_http_client_3xx_redirect_current_behavior_snapshot` will
   fail — update it to assert `resp.status == 302` and rename to drop
   the "current-behavior-snapshot" suffix. Rename to
   `distributed_http_client_returns_3xx_redirect_as_ok_response`.
2. Add a second test asserting that a 302 → a different internal host
   is ALSO observable (not followed) — use a second TcpListener in
   the test so the "Location" resolves but the client still doesn't
   follow it.

## Out of scope
- Building a full redirect-following mechanism (if callers want redirects,
  they can opt in explicitly by following the Location themselves after
  inspecting it).
- The asupersync HTTP client's default RedirectPolicy — that's a library
  default and changing it would affect every other caller. The fix is
  to override at the FrankenTerm distributed-client construction site.

## References
- Finding tick: ft-xbnl0.2.4 tick 349 (commit 4013f6e9)
- Related doc: docs/ft-xbnl0-2-4-completion-evidence.md §5 (out of scope carve-out)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"jemanuel","created_at":"2026-04-18T16:59:46.872931Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-18T17:08:05.852811Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T17:08:05.852502Z","close_reason":"Completed by tick 351 (commit 4717c434): DistributedHttpClient::new() now constructs via builder with .no_redirects() explicitly. 3xx responses are Ok(Response{status: 3xx}) observable to caller. Snapshot test renamed + flipped to assert Ok(302). All 40 ft-xbnl0.2.4 tests still pass via scripts/check_ft_xbnl0_2_4.sh.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["asupersync","distributed","http","security"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-kfkyi","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.2.4","type":"related","created_at":"2026-04-18T16:59:50.751738Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-n2j5w","title":"Fix MCP module pre-existing test errors under default features and verify 170 test functions pass","description":"# Goal
Fix the known test compilation/runtime errors in mcp.rs that prevent the 170 test functions from running with default features enabled.

# Background
MEMORY.md documents: mcp.rs has pre-existing test errors with default features. The MCP module (3,229 lines, 170 #[test] functions) has comprehensive test coverage. MCP submodules (mcp_bridge, mcp_framework, mcp_helpers, etc.) are declared inside mcp.rs via #[path], NOT in lib.rs — this means feature-gate interactions may affect module resolution.

# Design
## Diagnostic steps
1. Run: cargo test -p frankenterm-core --lib mcp 2>&1 | tee /tmp/mcp_errors.log
   (Using build recipe: CC=/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang CXX=/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang++ CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-<agent>-target)
2. Categorize errors:
   - Type mismatches from recent module changes -> update test expectations
   - Missing types/imports due to feature gates -> add #[cfg(feature)] or fix import paths
   - Async runtime errors (#[tokio::test] vs asupersync) -> migrate to sync or runtime_compat
   - Stale test data referencing old API shapes -> update fixtures
3. Fix each error, verifying after each fix that the change compiles
4. Final run: cargo test -p frankenterm-core --lib mcp -- all 170 tests green

## Common failure patterns to watch for
- MCP submodules use #[path] declarations — if a submodule file moves or renames, the path attribute breaks
- The 57 tokio-based test failures (ft-22x4r) may overlap — if MCP tests use #[tokio::test], coordinate with ft-682n6
- Feature flag interactions: some test helper types may only exist under specific features (mcp, subprocess-bridge, etc.)
- robot_types.rs changes may have updated MCP response envelope shapes without updating mcp.rs test expectations

## Structured Logging
- Diagnostic log: mcp_test_fix_<timestamp>.log with each error, category, fix applied
- Before/after test counts to verify no tests lost

# Acceptance Criteria
1. cargo test -p frankenterm-core --lib mcp passes with 0 failures under default features.
2. All 170 test functions execute (none skipped, none #[ignore]d without documented reason).
3. No semantic changes to test intent — only update expectations to match current API.
4. Diagnostic log documenting each fix for future reference.
5. If any tests require non-default features, they must have explicit #[cfg(feature = "...")] annotations with comments explaining why.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":120,"created_at":"2026-04-06T18:58:08.516219Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-07T03:18:06.705038Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T03:18:06.704865Z","close_reason":"Verified: 201 MCP tests pass with zero failures under default features (cargo test -p frankenterm-core --lib mcp::tests). The pre-existing test errors documented in memory were likely fixed by other agents. No code changes needed — the 170 test functions mentioned in the bead have grown to 201, all passing.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fix","mcp","testing"]}
